rulings.Case Title: EILEEN TATING VS. JPMORGAN CHASE, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Strike Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 1. Defendant'S Motion To Strike Second Amended Complaint Denied. Newly alleged causes of action arise out the same operative facts. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: EILEEN TATING VS. JPMORGAN CHASE, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., AS SUCCESSOR BY, EMC MORTGAGE LLC, FORMERLY KNOWN AS EMC MORTGAGE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend as follows: (1) first cause of action for fraud to allege damages; (2) second and third causes of action with final leave to amend to allege facts re: duty. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: GWYN LI JUN MIAO et al VS. MARIA T. LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Orders
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFFs GWYN MIAO and HONGXIU HE's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Obey Court Orders Continued to March 19, 2014. Defendants must provide complete responses to form interrogatory 15.1, i.e. identifying each denial and affirmative defense and then providing responses for subsections (a), (b) and (c) as to each denial and affirmative defense. Responses must be hand-delivered to Plaintiff's counsel and courtesy copies must be delivered to department 501 no later than March 11, 2014. Plaintiff may file a reply no later than March 14, 2014. Monetary sanctions are granted in the amount of $2,095. If complete responses are not timely provided motion for terminating sanctions will be granted. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TIERNEY VS. NASIR JAVAID et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT NASIR JAVAID, DEJAUNA JOSEPH MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain/shift burden and show that Plaintiff's causes of action 1 though 5 arise out of protected activity. See Wang v. Wal-Mart Real Estate Business Trust (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 790; Personal Court Reporters v. Rand (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 182. Plaintiff's objections to Defendants' request for judicial notice are sustained. Defendant's motion is frivolous within the meaning of CCP 128.5. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $11,245.50. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TIERNEY VS. NASIR JAVAID et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANTs NASIR JAVAID and DEJAUNA JOSEPH DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled. Each cause of action is based at least in part on Defendant's actions in 2013 (marketing of property for sale and asbestos inspection). Defendant failed to show that Defendant's actions giving rise to Plaintiff's causes of action actions are not privileged within the meaning of CCC 47. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TIERNEY VS. NASIR JAVAID et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 3. PLAINTIFFs JOSEPH TIERNEY and 376 CASTRO STREET, LLC,'s Motion For Preliminary Injunction GRANTED. Moving party sustained its burden. Defendants are enjoined from denying access to the property to the co-owner for asbestos inspection. Defendant are enjoined from transferring ownership interest to third parties or encumbering the property pending resolution of this action. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHE SCHREIBER VS. ACA INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANTs PAM MONIZ and DMA CLAIMS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting allegations of conspiracy/agency/aiding and abetting, or to allege facts supporting each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendants. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANSEN, JORY, ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF VS. ALEX MORA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Restric Public Access To Court Records And Register Of Actions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT ALEX MORA's Motion To Restrict Public Access To Court Records And Register Of Actions GRANTED. Record shall be restricted per CCP 1161.2. Record shall not be sealed. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: IRMA ENCINAS VS. OONA HANAWALT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANTs OONA HANAWALT and B. ALISON PANKO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Retaliation, waiver and estoppel are issues of fact. Triable issue of fact as to purported undisputed fact #4. = (501/JAR)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK VS. RANDALL B. ALLISON et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2014, line 7. PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Continued to April 17, 2014 per Court's March 3, 2014 order. = (501/JAR)
